channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Federal judge rules Trump administration cannot immediately deport Tufts student
item.description: A federal judge recently ruled a Tufts University grad student cannot be immediately deported. Meanwhile, ICE agents made their latest student detainment near the University of Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump commutes the sentence of Ozy Media co-founder Carlos Watson in financial conspiracy case
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump commuted the sentence of Ozy Media co-founder Carlos Watson on Friday, just hours before Watson was due to report to prison for a nearly 10-year sentence in a financial conspiracy case.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: At least 4 dead after severe storms in the South Texas-Mexico border region and hundreds rescued
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Drenching rains along the Texas-Mexico border trapped hundreds of people in flooded homes and in cars stranded in high waters, scrambling rescue crews to calls for help that continued Friday even as the downpours let up. At least four people died, including some who drowned.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ and JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oregon city at heart of high court homelessness ruling blocked from enforcing camping ban
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — An Oregon judge issued a preliminary injunction Friday blocking the city at the heart of a U.S. Supreme Court ruling on homeless encampments from enforcing its camping rules unless it meets certain conditions, as part of a lawsuit filed by advocates.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Noem ends her first visit to Latin America in Mexico to discuss crime and migration
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — U.S. Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem landed in Mexico on Friday to cap off a tour to three Latin American nations to discuss immigration, crime and deportation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Columbia University interim president steps down amid clash with Trump admin over federal funding
item.description: Columbia University Interim President Katrina Armstrong is stepping down from her role leading the school, with board of trustees co-chair Claire Shipman taking her place.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Voice of America wins in court, for now, as judge blocks Trump administration from firing staff
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Voice of America can’t be silenced just yet. A federal judge on Friday halted the Trump administration’s efforts to dismantle the eight-decade-old U.S. government-funded international news service, calling the move a “classic case of arbitrary and capricious decision making.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk says xAI buys social media platform X for $45 billion
item.description: Elon Musk said on Friday his artificial intelligence firm xAI has bought his social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Wisconsin attorney general sues Elon Musk to block $1 million payment offers
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Wisconsin’s Democratic attorney general asked a court on Friday to block billionaire Elon Musk from handing out $1 million checks to voters this weekend, just days before the state’s hotly contested Supreme Court race was to be decided.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: US Naval Academy ends affirmative action in admissions
item.description: The U.S. Naval Academy will no longer consider race, ethnicity or sex as a factor for admission to the service academy.
item.dc:creator: Brian Witte, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Consumer sentiment falls to lowest level in over two years
item.description: The University of Michigan's Survey of Consumers found a nearly 12% drop in consumer sentiment to 57 in March.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Second South Carolina inmate chooses execution by firing squad
item.description: A second South Carolina death row inmate, Mikal Mahdi, 41, has chosen execution by firing squad on April 11 following his guilty murder plea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top-rated seafood restaurant in every state: Report
item.description: There are plenty of fish in the sea, as the old saying goes. Unfortunately, there are far fewer exceptional chefs who can cook one for dinner.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Newsom-backed San Francisco speed camera program fines certain drivers more than others
item.description: San Francisco launched a pilot program approved by California Gov. Gavin Newsom that allows the city to issue fines to speeding drivers based on income.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) warned in a speech Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline if they agree to a deal that achieves only “illusory peace.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: A mayor's lawsuit shut down a newspaper over fake stories. Now he faces corruption allegations
item.description: EVERETT, Mass. (AP) — For years, the mayor of a Boston suburb dreaded Wednesdays. That was the day when a local weekly would publish shocking allegations that he was on the take, sexually harassing women or under investigation by the FBI.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A second South Carolina death row inmate chooses execution by firing squad
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina death row inmate on Friday chose execution by firing squad, just five weeks after the state carried out its first death by bullets. Mikal Mahdi, who pleaded guilty to murder for killing a police officer in 2004, is scheduled to be executed April 11.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate Republican chair challenges Trump over emergency funding move
item.description: Sen. Susan Collins (R-Maine), the head of the powerful Senate Appropriations Committee, and her Democratic counterpart are challenging the Trump administration’s handling of emergency funding included in legislation passed earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Yosemite National Park employee dies from mysterious injuries in staff housing
item.description: Multiple federal agencies are investigating a female park employee's suspicious death after she was found suffering from severe injuries inside Yosemite National Park.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Snake covered by Endangered Species Act killed at Michigan park
item.description: Officials are urging “understanding” over “fear” after a federally protected snake was recently found killed at the Matthaei Botanical Gardens and Nichols Arboretum in Ann Arbor.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Idaho murders suspect Bryan Kohberger penned essay showing he knew how to cover tracks
item.description: Bryan Kohberger wrote an essay on how to handle a crime scene investigation while studying at DeSales University, two years before the Idaho student murders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Poll of scientists says large majority weighing leaving US
item.description: The scientific journal Nature poll found that 75.3 percent of scientists are considering leaving the U.S. after the administration cut funding for research.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: As many as 6 hurricanes forecast to strike US this season
item.description: “It only takes one landfall to create a devastating season,” AccuWeather chief meteorologist Jonathan Porter said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Rubio says at least 300 foreign students' visas have been revoked
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Thursday at least 300 foreign students have had their visas revoked amid the Trump administration's immigration agenda, a far higher number than was previously known.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: Sundance Film Festival is leaving Utah for Colorado, starting in 2027
item.description: Boulder has officially been unveiled as the city to host the Sundance Film Festival starting in 2027, according to a press release from the Sundance Institute.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NY county clerk refuses to file Texas' fine for doctor accused of prescribing abortion pills
item.description: A county clerk in New York refused Thursday to file a more than $100,000 judgment from Texas against a doctor accused of prescribing abortion pills to a woman near Dallas, setting up a potential challenge to laws designed to shield abortion providers who serve patients in states with abortion bans.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Toddler sneaks onto White House grounds
item.description: A toddler slipped through the White House fence on Wednesday, the Secret Service said in a post on the social platform X. “Just after 6:30 p.m. on Wednesday, U.S. Secret Service Uniformed Division officers observed a child slip through the White House north fence.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KTLA 5

item.title: DC-bound Amtrak passenger may have exposed travelers to measles, health officials say
item.description: The passenger rode an Amtrak train in the Northeast last week before visiting an urgent care center in DC, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Trump admin potential travel ban concerning airlines: Editor
item.description: "Even the announcement that they're being proposed on the list is sending a chill through the travel industry.
item.dc:creator: Markie Martin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ex-NFL player charged after 190 dogs seized from dog fighting venture
item.description: An Oklahoma man is facing federal charges after officials seized 190 dogs from an animal fighting venture, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Doctor accused of trying to kill his wife during hike
item.description: Police say the man tried to push his wife off the trail and allegedly hit her in the head multiple times with a rock.
item.dc:creator: Jill Kuramoto, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden rule requiring serial numbers and background checks for ghost guns
item.description: The Supreme Court on Wednesday upheld a Biden administration regulation on the nearly impossible-to-trace weapons called ghost guns, clearing the way for continued serial numbers, background checks and age verification requirements to buy them in kits online.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court upholds Biden’s ‘ghost guns’ crackdown
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld the Biden administration’s regulation of “ghost guns” in a 7-2 decision Wednesday, rejecting a challenge brought by gun rights groups and several manufacturers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, ABC 4

item.title: Vance to accompany wife to Greenland amid backlash
item.description: Vance’s announcement adds another layer of tension to an already fraught atmosphere surrounding his wife’s planned visit.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: How the Signal 'group chat' furor has affected the 5 biggest players
item.description: The fact that such inherently sensitive information was being communicated in such a forum — and in front of a reporter whom no other participant appears to have noticed — has caused a political storm.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, Fox 5

item.title: The MLB teams with the highest
item.description: A California team has the highest median ticket price, a new report found, but they don't play in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Delta Air Lines unveils 2025 trading cards to celebrate 100 years
item.description: Trading card collectors and aviation fanatics will soon have an exclusive set of cards to begin collecting on Delta Air Lines flights this year.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, ABC 4
